methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $70.5B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Ares Industries is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $31M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers: 8 private companies at Series B stage within Defense & Security, plus 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). Private peers include Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), and Castelion and Defense Unicorns (both $1.0B), with a peer range spanning $125M to $3.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Peer-Median approach uses the median valuation across the 8 private comparables, yielding $1.0B. The Stage-Progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to a $70.5B total-raised proxy, implying $141.0B at the next stage. This latter figure warrants scrutiny given the significant gap versus peer medians and appears disconnected from the company's current fundraising trajectory and peer set sizing.

The triangulated range is $850M (low) to $162.2B (high), with a median of $71.0B. The defensible valuation band centers on the peer-median approach at $1.0B, supported by stage and sector comparables. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of the next financing event, differentiation relative to named competitors (Anduril, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic), and whether the $70.5B total-raised proxy reflects an appropriate baseline. A $850M–$1.2B range appears most consistent with available peer data.
